pattern: Sorting the values and counting, for each value, every factor pair whose product equals it builds the number of trees rooted at each value by multiplying subtree counts.
hints: Every value can be a single-node tree, and any value equal to a product of two listed values can also be an internal node.
Process values in increasing order so that whenever you form a value as a product, the counts for its two factors are already known.
Let f[v] be the number of trees with root v; start at 1 and for each ordered factor pair (a, b) with a*b = v add f[a]*f[b]; sum all f[v] modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: first example, one composite enables a small tree
second example, two factor pairs for ten
single value, only the trivial tree
no value is a product of others, every count is one
chained powers of two compound the counts
a perfect square has a single equal-factor pair
unsorted input must still be handled

stress.description: products built from a base set so many internal nodes appear
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    base = [2, 3, 5, 7]
    vals = set(base)
    cur = list(base)
    while len(vals) < max(1, min(size, 60)):
        a = rng.choice(cur)
        b = rng.choice(cur)
        p = a * b
        if p <= 10**9:
            vals.add(p)
            cur.append(p)
        else:
            cur = list(vals)
    out = list(vals)
    rng.shuffle(out)
    return [out]
stress.note: constructed products maximise factor pairs to exercise the dp
